name: Sophia Jones
title: PhD Candidate – Reinforcement Learning

education.degree: PhD in Accountable, Responsible, & Transparent AI
details: Researching reinforcement learning with graph based methods in the UKRI CDT for Accountable, Responsible, and Transparent AI, under the supervision of Prof Özgür Şimşek.
Research aims include achieving more efficient value function approximation, and agent explainability at a policy level.
Achieved 75% in the integrated MRes year.
Organise and host a popular reading group for the AI and ML department
Studied modules relating to AI and theoretical computer science.
Dissertation judged outstanding. Contributed novel research in the field of randomised algorithms on graphs.
Studied courses in pure mathematics and statistics.
Won the Deb Bose Prize for Best Mathematical Dissertation.
Elected representative for students on my course.

projects: Created a forecasting system to estimate the optimal delivery fleet size, achieving over 90% accuracy.
Developed a novel algorithm using routing heuristics to find geographically optimal locations for delivery hubs.

workExperience.details: Developed models to predict the total loss the bank may incur due to customers defaulting on mortgage payments.
Consulted on an explainable credit risk prediction project.

tutoring: Discrete Mathematics and Mathematical Reasoning (University of Edinburgh)
courseCoLeader: Data Science with Python (Volunteer at Code Your Dreams)
mscSupervision: Comparison of Handcrafted and Autonomously Learned Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning Agents in Tetris (University of Bath)
Using Curriculum Learning Techniques with Deep Reinforcement Learning in Tetris (University of Bath)
Reinforcement Learning for Tetris using Options (University of Bath)

awards: Deb Bose Prize for Best Mathematical Dissertation (2015)
